message:
fix: resolve robot surface session lookup and stale model metadata

Two fixes:

1. --robot-tail and --robot-activity now resolve session names through
   the same prefix-match logic that list/status use, so labeled sessions
   (e.g. myproject--repro) are found correctly. Previously these robot
   surfaces called tmux.SessionExists() with the raw name, skipping
   resolution. Also added robotActivity to needsConfigLoading.

2. modelNameForPane() in both cli and robot packages now reads fallback
   model names from config.DefaultModels() instead of hardcoded strings
   that had drifted out of sync (gpt-4 → gpt-5.3-codex, etc.).

Closes #104
Closes #105

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1709,15 +1709,18 @@ func modelNameForPane(p tmux.Pane) string {
 			}
 		}
 	}
+	// Fall back to compiled-in defaults from config.DefaultModels() so the
+	// values stay in sync with the canonical defaults. (ntm#105)
+	defaults := config.DefaultModels()
 	switch agentType {
 	case tmux.AgentClaude:
-		return "claude-sonnet-4-6"
+		return defaults.DefaultClaude
 	case tmux.AgentCodex:
-		return "gpt-4"
+		return defaults.DefaultCodex
 	case tmux.AgentGemini:
-		return "gemini-2.0-flash"
+		return defaults.DefaultGemini
 	case tmux.AgentOllama:
-		return "llama3"
+		return defaults.DefaultOllama
 	default:
 		return ""
 	}
